agency_name: Inspector-General of Taxation and Taxation Ombudsman (IGTO)
executive_summary: The IGTO is a micro statutory integrity agency (around 29 staff) that investigates taxpayer complaints/disputes about the ATO and Tax Practitioners Board and conducts systemic review investigations. The plan candidly admits acute resource pressure: a growing backlog of complex disputes, an 'unacceptably high' nine-month wait time, and forecast 5-10% annual growth in disputes as the ATO resumes compliance and debt-collection activity. Its single largest current initiative is implementing a new case management system (CMS) to digitally transform investigation services, alongside building an integrity framework to meet new NACC obligations and refreshing ATO protocols. As a Treasury-portfolio regulator/oversight body, demand fits data/analytics, digital delivery (CMS), governance/integrity, and workforce capability — but all opportunities must be right-sized to a tiny appropriation-funded agency that already outsources ICT and internal audit. A Senate inquiry into the IGTO's own performance adds a credible governance/capability signal.

strategic_priorities.title: Transform dispute investigation via a new case management system
strategic_priorities.description: Implement a modern, secure CMS giving complainants a real-time portal to track disputes, improving transparency on delays, caseload monitoring and reporting; later expanded to review investigations, stakeholder and information management.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: As a key current initiative, we are implementing a new case management system (CMS) which will digitally transform our investigation services.

strategic_priorities.title: Reduce backlog and improve timeliness of dispute investigations
strategic_priorities.description: Address an 'unacceptably high' nine-month wait time and a growing backlog of complex/highly complex disputes, including via the Feb 2023 joint IGTO-ATO triage/assessment process, while protecting staff wellbeing.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: The current wait time for an IGTO dispute investigation remains unacceptably high - nine-months.

strategic_priorities.title: Foster fairness, integrity and accountability (NACC obligations)
strategic_priorities.description: Develop and implement an integrity framework and policies/processes to discharge obligations under the National Anti-Corruption Commission framework, as a named Commonwealth integrity agency from 1 July 2023.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: the IGTO has considered how best to reallocate resources to develop policies and processes to ensure that we meet the obligations under the NACC legislative framework.

strategic_priorities.title: Improve data systems and capability to capture and report evidence
strategic_priorities.description: Improve information and data capture, analysis and reporting to deliver meaningful reports and briefings that inform recommendations for improvement.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: Improve our systems information and data capture, analysis and reporting capabilities to deliver meaningful reports and briefings.

strategic_priorities.title: Improve community awareness of IGTO services
strategic_priorities.description: Raise awareness of the office, especially among vulnerable taxpayers and those outside metropolitan areas, via multi-channel communications and stakeholder leverage.
strategic_priorities.source_quote: An important priority and persistent challenge for the IGTO is to improve the community's awareness of our office and the services we deliver.

strategic_priorities.title: Refresh protocols and guidelines with the ATO
strategic_priorities.description: Refresh ATO protocols/guidelines to better communicate expectations, support effective investigations and enable escalation of significant matters to senior officers.

strategic_priorities.title: Attract and retain tax/superannuation specialists and build resilience
strategic_priorities.description: Establish IGTO as an employer of choice for scarce tax/super specialist and investigative talent, with training to AGIS standards and wellbeing support for staff in high-stress investigations.

reform_and_change_drivers: ATO resumption of compliance and debt-collection activities post-COVID, expected to drive a 5-10% annual rise in disputes (debt complaints were historically ~24% of receipts).
New NACC operations from 1 July 2023 making the IGTO a named Commonwealth integrity agency with new compliance obligations.
Economic pressures (high inflation, interest rates, debt, insolvency risk) increasing debt- and superannuation-related disputes.
ATO disclosure of tax debt to Credit Reporting Bureaus, which is paused where a dispute is lodged with IGTO - a direct workload driver.
Shift toward Complex and Highly Complex disputes that are resource- and time-intensive.
New CMS rollout (FY24) as a digital transformation of investigation services.
Planned abolition/replacement of the AAT affecting referral powers.
challenges_and_risks: Nine-month dispute wait time and growing backlog of complex investigations against a 29-person workforce.
Micro-agency carrying the same mandatory corporate/compliance and reporting burden as the largest APS agencies.
Appropriation-dependent funding outside the agency's control, limiting capacity to scale.
Workforce risk: attracting and retaining scarce tax/super specialists; staff wellbeing in emotionally demanding roles (Key Risk 4).
Dependence on ATO for independent access to records/systems, with no sanction for non-compliance under the IGT Act.
Declining complainant survey results (59% service satisfaction in FY23 against a 70% target).
Reliance on external providers for ICT and internal audit.
Governance/effectiveness scrutiny via a Senate inquiry into IGTO performance.

news_and_controversies.summary: The IGTO engaged publicly on the ATO's handling of re-raised historical ('on hold') tax debts, a live systemic issue in 2024.
news_and_controversies.implication: A current systemic-review topic with stakeholder-communication and data-analysis dimensions where review-support could be relevant; confirm scope before approaching.

news_and_controversies.summary: Trade press reported the ANAO being urged to audit ATO tax-debt reporting - adjacent to, not directly about, the IGTO.
news_and_controversies.implication: Indicates sustained scrutiny pressure across the IGTO/ATO oversight space; useful context rather than a direct IGTO issue.

anao_findings.summary: ANAO performance audit 'Management of Complaints by the Australian Taxation Office' examines the ATO's complaints handling - directly adjacent to the IGTO's role investigating ATO complaints. Specific findings naming the IGTO were not confirmed.
anao_findings.implication: The ATO/IGTO complaints interface is an audited, scrutinised area - potential for process-improvement, data and assurance work; confirm whether IGTO is named in recommendations before pitching.

parliamentary_matters.summary: A Senate Economics References Committee inquiry titled 'Performance of the Inspector-General of Taxation' examined the agency's own effectiveness, independence and capability.
parliamentary_matters.implication: Primary parliamentary record on the agency's effectiveness - essential reading for any governance, capability-review or assurance pitch; findings may directly create demand.

parliamentary_matters.summary: The IGTO appeared before the Joint Committee of Public Accounts and Audit (JCPAA) in June 2025.
parliamentary_matters.implication: Recent parliamentary engagement; Hansard may reveal funding, workload or performance pressures useful for shaping a proportionate offer.

opportunity_areas.area: New case management system - delivery and implementation
opportunity_areas.agency_need: FY24 rollout of a new CMS to digitally transform investigation services, with a real-time complainant portal and improved caseload monitoring and reporting; later expansion to review investigations and information management.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: Discovery and service design plus solutions implementation/integration support for the CMS and complainant portal, sized for a micro-agency that already uses third-party ICT providers - including business/process analysis and service transition support.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan names the CMS as the 'key current initiative' to 'digitally transform our investigation services' and lists specific functional outcomes (real-time portal, delay transparency, caseload analytics), making this concrete and time-bound.

opportunity_areas.area: Data capture, analytics and reporting capability
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Strategic priority to improve systems information and data capture, analysis and reporting to inform recommendations, plus identifying systemic issues from complaint/dispute trends (KPA 2).
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: An information/data strategy and business-intelligence build to turn complaint and dispute data into trend analysis and reporting that informs own-initiative/review topic selection - a short framework/design engagement aligned to the CMS data.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan explicitly prioritises improving data capture, analysis and reporting, and ties review-topic selection to analysis of complaint and dispute trends; the new CMS provides the data foundation.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: As a named Commonwealth integrity agency since 1 July 2023, IGTO must develop and implement a fit-for-purpose integrity framework and supporting policies/processes under the NACC framework.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A short advisory to design/uplift the integrity framework, governance policies and escalation processes aligned to NACC obligations, building on the work of its newly engaged Integrity Officer.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan names integrity-framework development as a strategic priority and a deliberate response to new NACC obligations - a clear, bounded governance need for a small agency.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: A Senate Economics References Committee inquiry into the IGTO's own performance signals scrutiny of effectiveness, independence and capability.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: An independent health check or governance assessment of the agency's operating model and dispute-service effectiveness, helping translate inquiry findings into an actionable response.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The external Senate inquiry into IGTO performance creates credible demand for independent assurance/governance support; right-sized to a short review for a micro-agency.

opportunity_areas.agency_need: Key Risk 4 and a strategic priority around attracting/retaining scarce tax/super specialists, AGIS-standard investigator training, and maintaining staff wellbeing during high-stress investigations.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A proportionate workforce plan and capability/retention strategy for specialist investigators, plus resilience/wellbeing program design - delivered as a short advisory given the small headcount.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan repeatedly names workforce capability, retention and resilience as priorities and risks, with declining survey results adding pressure; offering must be modest in scale.

opportunity_areas.area: Dispute investigation process and timeliness improvement
opportunity_areas.agency_need: Backlog reduction and timeliness against a nine-month wait time, with continuous improvement of the dispute investigation service and the IGTO-ATO triage process.
opportunity_areas.suggested_offering: A short process-improvement/change engagement to streamline the dispute workflow and embed the new CMS-enabled triage, focused on cycle-time reduction and benefits tracking.
opportunity_areas.rationale: The plan candidly flags an unacceptable wait time and backlog and commits to continuously optimising the dispute service and ICT - a real but general improvement need closely tied to the CMS.

recommended_actions.action: Approach the IGTO executive (and the Assistant Director - Risk, Governance and Compliance) with a right-sized CMS implementation/integration and complainant-portal proposition, positioned around the FY24 rollout.
recommended_actions.rationale: The CMS is the named flagship initiative; a micro-agency that outsources ICT will need delivery and integration support, and timing aligns with the rollout window.

recommended_actions.action: Prepare a short capability statement on turning complaint/dispute data into trend analytics and reporting, referencing KPA 2 and the CMS data foundation.
recommended_actions.rationale: Data capture, analysis and reporting is an explicit priority and underpins systemic review-topic selection; a small, well-scoped BI engagement is credible.

recommended_actions.action: Read the Senate Economics References Committee 'Performance of the IGTO' inquiry report and the June 2025 JCPAA Hansard before any governance/assurance pitch.
recommended_actions.rationale: These primary sources reveal effectiveness, funding and capability concerns and determine whether a governance health-check is timely and welcome.

recommended_actions.action: Develop a modest NACC-aligned integrity-framework advisory offer to support the agency's Integrity Officer.
recommended_actions.rationale: New NACC obligations are a named, time-relevant governance need where a focused short advisory fits a micro-agency budget.

recommended_actions.action: Watch for procurement signals around the CMS expansion (to review investigations, stakeholder and information management) and any funding outcomes from Treasury/Budget.
recommended_actions.rationale: The plan signals staged CMS expansion; appropriation-dependent funding means timing of any engagement hinges on budget decisions.

engagement_targets: Inspector-General of Taxation / Taxation Ombudsman (Accountable Authority) and the senior executive team
Assistant Director - Risk, Governance and Compliance (risk, governance, NACC/integrity)
Integrity Officer (NACC obligations and integrity framework)
ICT / digital function and the CMS project owner (noting reliance on external ICT providers)
Independent Audit and Risk Committee (assurance and governance matters)
talking_points: Your CMS rollout is the agency's flagship transformation - we can provide proportionate implementation, integration and portal delivery for a micro-agency that relies on external ICT providers, without over-building.
The nine-month wait time and complex-dispute backlog are solvable with CMS-enabled triage and a focused process-improvement effort tied to measurable cycle-time reduction.
KPA 2 depends on turning complaint and dispute data into trend insight - we can stand up a lean analytics and reporting capability on top of your new CMS data.
New NACC obligations create a clear, bounded need to design an integrity framework and escalation processes - we can support your Integrity Officer with a short, practical advisory.
With a Senate inquiry examining IGTO performance, an independent operating-model and dispute-service health check can help you translate findings into a credible response.
Everything we propose is right-sized to a 29-person appropriation-funded agency - short advisories and staged delivery, not large programs.
caveats: This is a micro-agency (around 29 staff) with appropriation-only funding and existing reliance on external ICT and internal-audit providers, so all opportunities must be modest and staged - large delivery or transformation programs are not realistic. The strongest, clearly-evidenced opportunity is CMS-related digital/data work; governance, integrity, workforce and assurance offers are inferred from stated priorities/risks and are smaller and lower-confidence. External research was truncated and surfaced no scandal-level issues; the Senate performance inquiry and adjacent ANAO complaints-handling audit are the main external signals and their detailed findings were not fully verified. The plan analysed is the 2024-2027 edition; a later corporate plan and recent annual report should be checked for current funding, staffing and CMS status before engaging.
